Kadiri

# Kadiri

[](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

![Ratnakar Sadasyula's avatar](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!R73I!,w_36,h_36,c_fill,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F81a2c542-8b3f-4ade-98d2-a40950cffbe3_2640x1980.jpeg)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!_UjH!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fafe2765a-2f16-461f-89d7-39633743d6e5_259x194.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!_UjH!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fafe2765a-2f16-461f-89d7-39633743d6e5_259x194.jpeg)

Kadiri, one of the famous Narasimha Kshetras in Andhra Pradesh, gets it's name from the fact that Shree Narasimha is believed to have resided here in the roots of a Kadiri(Mulberry) tree for a long time. Also at Sthothadri hill nearby Prahalada is believed to have prayed to Narasimha to calm him down after he killed Hiranyakasipu.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!wod3!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fd1fb7719-48cf-4a4f-b5e5-af293c4ef360_283x178.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!wod3!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fd1fb7719-48cf-4a4f-b5e5-af293c4ef360_283x178.jpeg)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!ZDlh!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F98be3028-a373-43b7-bfd2-952fb9a42e7c_259x194.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!ZDlh!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F98be3028-a373-43b7-bfd2-952fb9a42e7c_259x194.jpeg)

Another explanation for the name of Kadiri, is that the Vishnu paadam( Kaa) was found on the Sthothadri hill( Adri). Also this is the only temple where Prahalada is worshipped along with Narasimha.

It's believed that the original murthi were found by Maharshi Bhrigu during Vasantha, hence the deities are referred to as Vasantha Vallabha Utsava Murthulu. Also it's believed Veda Vyasa had established a gurukul here. Arjuna is believed to have done penance on the banks of the Madilleru river here, which connects the holy tanks- Brighu Theertham, Sesha Theertham, Lakshmi Theertham, Vyaasa Theertham, Kunthi Theertham, Draupadi Theertham around the temple

The construction of the temple at Kadiri was started by Bukka Raya who built the Gopuram here around 1353 AD, while Harihara Raya, made further additions between 1356-1418. And Sri Krishnadeva Raya renovated it during 1509-29 AD. It is known for it's long pillars within the temple compound used for lighting lamps built by Narasimha and Lakshmana Dasari, while Sri Krishnadeva Raya got the Ranga Mantapam constructed with it's lion faces, and a shrine for the Alwars.

Achyuta Deva Raya later added the Eastern Raja Gopuram, in 1545, as well as a shrine for Ganesha, Krishna, while Tirumala Raya, got the corridors and Pushkarni built. There is also a Mahishura Mardhini shrine in the main complex, that was constructed by Chattrapathi Shivaji Maharaj when he visited Kadiri around 1642-44. The gold ornaments were donated by Kopanna who was working with the Wodeyars.

The Kadiri temple also has a 1000 pillar mandapam built by the local chieftain of Tadipatri. The temple draws devotees not just from Andhra Pradesh, but many from Karnataka, Tamil Nadu too especially during the Brahmotsavams here.

The very popular Telugu folk song Bet Rayi Swami Devuda, Nanellinoda, Katamarayuda, Kadiri Narasimhudaa, that references Kadiri, is actually a a reference to the Dasavatharams in the Rayalaseema regional dialect.

The song actually starts with *"Betarayi Swami Devuda, Nanellinoda, Betarayi Swami Devuda. Katama Rayuda, Kadiri Narasimhudaa Metaina Vetagada ninne nammithi raa"* And then proceeds to describe each of the Dasavatarams.

*Betarayi Swami Devuda, Nanellinoda basically meaning Lord of Hunting, who rules over me.*

*Katama Rayudu is believed to be a local chieftain of Kadiri, known for his valour.*

*Metaina Vetagada ninne nammithi raa meaning I place my trust in you the best of hunters.*

The hunter reference here is to how Narasimha managed to hunt down Hiranyakasipu, using all his tact and intelligence, and finally slay him.

# An error occurred.

Unable to execute JavaScript.

This is that folk song in full, sung by the maestro Mangalampalli Balamurali Krishna.

The Narasimha Swamy Ratha Yatra at Kadiri during Sankranti, when he is accompanied by his consorts Sridevi, Bhudevi on a massive Ratha with 6 wheels, weighing 120 tonnes, draws large crowds of devotees from Andhra, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!hI2n!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Ffc752735-d116-4098-9eb3-8b896ed3b65c_286x176.jpeg)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!oBDD!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fffec860c-69ed-4df2-87a8-b8804ad50ef5_259x194.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!oBDD!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fffec860c-69ed-4df2-87a8-b8804ad50ef5_259x194.jpeg)

Timmamma Marrimanu located around 35 km from Kadiri, was listed in Guiness Book in 1989, spreading over an area of 5 sq acress. And is a very popular attraction to those visiting Kadiri. It's believed that Thimmamamba, the daughter of Venkatappa, Mangamamba, commited Sati when her husband Veerayya passed away. The Banyan tree is believed to have sprung at the North East corner of that site.

The massive banyan tree was listed by Guinness in 1989 as the largest ever, due to efforts of one Satyanarayana. Currently it covers around 8.5 acres with around 6900 roots hanging from it. The Thimmamma Marrimanu is around 660 years old, and resembles a mini forest by itself. Many believe this banyan tree, to represent Thimmamma herself and many childless couples visit this tree and pray for children.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!PUnj!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F37f46704-f947-4e18-9a1b-f02e76445e70_480x385.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Also around 12 km from Kadiri at Katurupalli is the Samadhi of Yogi Vemana, the great saint,philosopher, thinker, whose sayings known as Vemana Padhyalu have become an integral part of Telugu culture.
